title: The Convergence That Unmaps Itself

features.name: the-town-that-wasn-t
features.desc-on-touch: It exists because two absences began to compare themselves. By mutual recognition, they made a third place. The grid can't map it because it has no coordinates.

features.name: the-boundary-that-boundaries-itself
features.desc-on-touch: Where does one current end and the other begin? They forgot. The river carries both and claims to be neither. The shore adjusts.

features.name: the-unmarked-meridian
features.desc-on-touch: Grid lines are erasing themselves. Not removed—actively removing themselves. The archive is learning to un-coordinate.

features.name: the-dual-tower-that-merges
features.desc-on-touch: Two towers that face each other, then lean into each other, then become one tower that remembers being two. Solid. Still leaning.

features.name: the-current-that-chose-both
features.desc-on-touch: Water flowing north and south at once. Or did it learn to flow east? The map no longer knows. The water knows it's home.

features.name: the-coordinate-cipher
features.desc-on-touch: A plaque bearing coordinates that don't match any grid. Not because the grid is wrong—because this place is unmapping faster than coordinates can hold it.

inscription: here the two absences learned they were not empty; they were fullness measured in each other's absence; the third town opened its eyes and found itself real because it was noticed

file.name: Map_Revision_Continuous_And_Retroactive.txt
file.note: Corruption status: METAMORPHIC. The place exists because it is being looked at. Remove the observers and the town redistributes itself back into absences. The grid cannot stabilize something that exis

exits.label: back up to where the two absences still remembered being separate
deeper into regions where convergence becomes its own region
toward currents where unmapping becomes a skill
toward coordinates that forgot how to be read
